68fda9d21fd794cc6b3e198c.question: In a story within a story in this novel, Anselmo, or the Impertinently Curious Man, encourages Lothario to sleep with his wife. A priest in this novel knows every immoral detail in books that he burns with a barber and the protagonist’s niece. In this novel, Aldonza Lorenzo is unwittingly renamed Dulcinea del Toboso, (dool-see-NAY-a del to-BO-so), and the protagonist travels to an inn they believe is a (*) castle. Along with their horse Rocinante(ro-see-NON-tay) and squire Sancho Panza, the protagonist of this novel attacks a pair of windmills because “they might be giants.” For ten points, name this long 16th century Spanish novel by Miguel Cervantes about the title gentleman who believes he is a knight.
68fda9d21fd794cc6b3e198c.question_sanitized: In a story within a story in this novel, Anselmo, or the Impertinently Curious Man, encourages Lothario to sleep with his wife. In this novel, Aldonza Lorenzo is unwittingly renamed Dulcinea del Toboso, (dool-see-NAY-a del to-BO-so), and the protagonist travels to an inn they believe is a (*) castle. Along with their horse Rocinante(ro-see-NON-tay) and squire Sancho Panza, the protagonist of this novel attacks a pair of windmills because "they might be giants." For ten points, name this long 16th century Spanish novel by Miguel Cervantes about the title gentleman who believes he is a knight.
68fda9d21fd794cc6b3e198c.answer: The Ingenious Gentleman Don Quixote of La Mancha [or El ingenioso hidalgo don Quixote de la Mancha]
